political.s: Coca-Cola contributed approximately $1.4 million in PAC and individual donations in 2024 and spent $4.9 million on lobbying efforts. The company shows balanced political engagement with no outside spending reported in 2024, reflecting a bipartisan approach to government influence.

charity.s: The Coca-Cola Foundation has awarded over $1.7 billion in grants over 40 years, focusing on water access, watershed conservation, recycling value chains, and disaster preparedness. The foundation matches employee donations and supports initiatives in education, empowerment, and environmental stewardship globally.
charity.focus: Water stewardship, watershed conservation, recycling value chains, disaster preparedness, education, economic prosperity

environment.s: Coca-Cola has achieved its water replenishment goal of returning 100% of water used in beverages since 2015, but faces criticism from environmental groups for scaling back sustainability commitments. Recent 2035 goals show reduced ambitions on recycled content (35-40% vs. prior 50%) and collection rates (70-75% vs. prior 100%), while the company increased single-use plastic bottle production by 9 billion from 2021-2022.

labor.s: Coca-Cola maintains comprehensive workplace safety policies and Supplier Guiding Principles requiring compliance with ILO standards and forced labor prevention. Employee relations reports indicate open communication and recognition programs, though the company faces historical criticism on supply chain labor practices.

dei.s: Coca-Cola firmly maintains DEI commitments while competitors scale back, setting goals for 50% women in senior leadership by 2030 and racial/ethnic representation reflecting census data. The company frames DEI as critical to business competitiveness and has resisted pressure from recent federal policy changes to abandon these initiatives.

animals.s: Not applicable to beverage manufacturing company.

guns.s: Not applicable to beverage manufacturing company.

privacy.s: Coca-Cola maintains privacy policies addressing data collection, cookies, and user consent with regional compliance frameworks. The company uses Google Analytics and targeted advertising technologies but provides privacy preference centers and data subject rights mechanisms, though some critics note transparency limitations.

execPay.s: CEO James Quincey earned $31.2 million in 2025, representing a 10.27% increase from 2024. The CEO-to-median worker pay ratio is not explicitly disclosed, but historical data shows top executives average $9 million annually while the company agreed to link executive pay to employee wage considerations.

sec.note: Pay ratio extracted from proxy filing — verify manually for accuracy
